reasoning:
Trump and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent have clearly stated their intention to announce a "shadow" Fed Chair well ahead of the end of Jerome Powell's term in May 2026. As of late October 2025, the shortlist has been narrowed to five candidates, and Bessent plans to present them to Trump for interviews "after Thanksgiving" (late November). Trump himself stated on October 27 that he anticipates naming a replacement "by the end of the year." 

This strongly indicates that an announcement in 2025 is the intended timeline, making it more likely than not that there WILL be an announcement (i.e. Trump will NOT refrain). However, the timeline is highly compressed: Thanksgiving is late November, leaving only about four weeks in December to conduct final interviews, complete any remaining vetting, and make a formal announcement amidst the holiday season. Furthermore, the phrasing that a decision "might be made" by year-end leaves explicit room for slippage into January 2026. Given Trump's mercurial nature and standard political delays, a ~35-40% chance of the announcement slipping past December 31 is highly justifiable. The market was pricing this at 42% just after the late-October news; I forecast 38%, slightly down-weighting the chance of slippage to reflect the very explicit, fast-tracked "shadow chair" strategy.
